headline: Storms clear Friday; check mildew and aphids this week
weather_notes: Today's 82% thunderstorm chance washes off any Bt applied now — hold pest treatments until Friday's dry window.
Wet-cool conditions (lows 37–39°F) following last week's warmth put all pea beds in peak powdery mildew risk territory.
Thursday night dips to 37°F — borderline stress for the tomato seedling in w2; check it Wednesday evening.
Rain through Thursday waters beds naturally; 63°F and sunny Friday is the window for outdoor tasks.

surfaced_tasks.title: Watch for powdery mildew
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today and the wet-cool week following warm dry days is the exact trigger pattern — all pea beds are 11 days from expected harvest and most vulnerable now.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for aphids — apply Bt if caterpillars present
surfaced_tasks.reason: Five days overdue across all three pea beds; inspect Friday after rain clears since today's storms would wash off any Bt immediately.

suggested_tasks.title: Check hardneck garlic for scapes
suggested_tasks.description: Late April is the emergence window in Zone 7b — snap scapes now to redirect energy to bulb development before they curl fully.
